accepted.reason: Raster conversion, one-hot encoding, and missing-value handling are core loop kernels with strong optimization value.
Phred-to-probability aggregation is a practical numeric reduction kernel relevant for speedup training.
Atmospheric pressure and latitude cross-section functions are concise physics kernels suitable for optimization tasks.
SoRec-style sigmoid/error-driven factor updates are dense numeric loops with clear performance relevance.
Simple integer summation loop is a compact baseline kernel useful for basic optimization behavior.

skipped.reason: Utility/decorator and unpack helpers are mostly orchestration glue with low standalone optimization signal.
Database/index population script is IO/service orchestration rather than compute-kernel optimization.
Depends heavily on CV/ML stack and application pipeline context, reducing portability for unpaired optimization tasks.
Large stochastic simulation with file IO and global mutable state is noisy and less suitable for stable training signal.
Descriptor/wrapper abstractions are interface-level plumbing rather than direct numeric optimization kernels.
